title: boronia barkeriana subsp. barkeriana
intro: Boronia barkeriana subsp. barkeriana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the citrus family, Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and leathery leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonoids and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Boronia barkeriana subsp. barkeriana are said to provide relief from fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, making it a popular remedy in traditional Australian medicine. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be effectively harnessed in herbal preparations.
